response:
Problems found
==============

1. Hard-coded secrets  
   • The OpenAI-looking API key (`sk-prod-abc123xyz`) and database password are literally in the source file.  
   • If this file is ever checked into VCS, pushed to GitHub/GitLab, attached to an issue, copied to a CI log, etc., the credentials are leaked.  
   • Anyone who can read the byte-code in production (“.pyc”) still gets the secret.

2. DEBUG=True in the same file  
   • Makes it very easy to forget to turn DEBUG off in production.  
   • When many frameworks (Flask, Django, FastAPI, etc.) run with DEBUG=True they:  
     – expose interactive tracebacks,  
     – return extra headers,  
     – disable some security middleware,  
     – may reload source code on the fly.

3. No separation of environments / single config class  
   • Production, staging, local-development all share one “Config”.  
   • Hard for tests to inject alternatives; you end up monkey-patching class attributes.

4. No typing, validation or defaults  
   • Typos like `DB_PASWORD` would silently fall back to class-variable lookup.  
   • Impossible to see at runtime whether a required variable is missing.

5. Key & password are not rotated or masked anywhere  
   • Once they land in logs or exception messages they remain readable.

6. Minor code-quality issues  
   • No doc-string; no constructor (`__init__`) if you ever wanted per-instance configs.  
   • Mutating class attributes at runtime changes the value for *all* imports of that module—can create surprising bugs in multi-threaded apps.

How to fix / improve
====================

A. Remove secrets from source control
-------------------------------------

• Strip the key/password; revoke and re-issue them.  
• Force-push removal history or use `git filter-repo` / `BFG Repo-Cleaner`.  
• Add `GIT_SECRETS`, `truffleHog`, or GitHub Secret Scanning in the CI pipeline.

B. Load secrets at runtime, not at build time
---------------------------------------------

Most Python projects use environment variables, a secret manager, or a vault.

Example with environment variables + pydantic (safe, validated):

```python
# settings.py
from pydantic import BaseSettings, Field, SecretStr

class Settings(BaseSettings):
    api_key:   SecretStr = Field(..., env="API_KEY")        # required
    db_pass:   SecretStr = Field(..., env="DB_PASSWORD")    # required
    debug:     bool       = Field(False, env="DEBUG")       # optional

    class Config:
        env_file = ".env"      # python-dotenv style (local dev only)
        case_sensitive = True

settings = Settings()          # will raise a ValidationError if missing
```

Usage:

```python
from settings import settings
print(settings.api_key.get_secret_value())
```

C. Isolate environment configurations
-------------------------------------

Create separate config objects:

```python
class BaseConfig(BaseSettings):
    debug: bool = False

class DevConfig(BaseConfig):
    debug: bool = True

class ProdConfig(BaseConfig):
    debug: bool = False
```

Select by `ENVIRONMENT` variable:

```python
def get_config():
    env = os.getenv("ENVIRONMENT", "dev").lower()
    return {"prod": ProdConfig, "dev": DevConfig}[env]()
```

D. Never ship DEBUG=True to production
--------------------------------------

• In CI/CD, enforce “pytest –-env=production” which asserts `DEBUG` is false.  
• Many frameworks (Flask: `FLASK_ENV=production`, Django: `DEBUG=False`) already read from env vars.

E. Use a secret-management service in production
-----------------------------------------------

AWS Secrets Manager, GCP Secret Manager, Azure Key Vault, Hashicorp Vault, Kubernetes Secrets. Example (pseudo):

```python
# aws_secret_loader.py
import boto3, json

def load_secret(name: str) -> dict:
    client = boto3.client('secretsmanager')
    resp   = client.get_secret_value(SecretId=name)
    return json.loads(resp['SecretString'])
```

F. Add static-analysis & runtime guards
--------------------------------------

• `bandit`, `pysa`, `semgrep`: detect hard-coded credentials.  
• `assert not settings.debug or app.env == "development"` right after boot.

G. General clean-up
-------------------

• Add docstring explaining each field.  
• Use `__all__` or `if TYPE_CHECKING` to limit what is imported.  
• Consider a `dataclass(frozen=True)` if you truly want immutability.

Recap
=====

– Hard-coded secrets are a critical security flaw.  
– DEBUG should never be enabled in production.  
– Load sensitive data from the runtime environment or a secret manager, validate it, and keep different envs isolated.  
– Add tooling and policy to prevent future leaks.
